text:
Signed up for service. 2 phones ported over. Gave them the account number which was incorrect the first time (comcast mobile has 2 account numbers, how stupid is that?) anyway finally got the right account number and port went through but the reps didnt give me any instructions and then the call just cust off and both phones are dead. I dont have a landline so i cant call support. Couldnt get to my friends house with the landline till the next day and then i called and they said they cant help me because they "cant validate my account because i cant receive a text message on my phone. Wow. Said i have to go to the store. I work till 9 so i cant go to the store. Now we are probably at about 3 hours on the phone with customer service so far and im really upset. Finally a manager or whoever says they will try to get my phone working and then the call cuts off. Noone called me back. they had my call back number. why dont they call me back? Tried to call back and 30 min hold time lol.
 
Is this how you treat your new customers because i now have an EXTREME hatred for tmobile and ive only had it for 1 day haha. The experience with your philipino/indonesian phone reps is BY FAR THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EXPERIENCE IVE EVER HAD IN MY ENTIRE LIFE. No offer of compensation. Nothing. Im calling tomorrow to get another transfer pin and cancelling service unless im given some substantial compensation for this torture.
 
Oh yea i was forced to leave work (boss is pissed) and drive to tmobile store which were very helpful and told me, yeah it usually doesnt work when you try to activate by phone lol. So why ddo you have it as an option? And how does it not work when you are  a multi trillion dollar corp? Lady at the store got it all working in about 10 mins.
 
Words cant dsescribe how bad this experience was. 0 out on 10. you get a Flat out F.

Worst customer service on the planet. Always upselling. Plenty of double-talking. Difficult to understand the reps. No one in CS appears to be U.S. based. This company is no better than AT&T and Verizon.

Changed from ATT to T-Mobile a week ago and after many issues with customer service, esims and poor connections I spoke to the store we purchased it from and advised I was not satisfied with their service and was going to return their product. I was told by the store I would get a refund(s) on the returned items and that I would not be charged a restocking fee. When one of the return invoices printed it reflected a $70 restocking fee. The store said they could not take it off but because the items were returned within the 7 day purchase period I should call their 1-800 number and request it from them directly. 800 told me the restock fee would not come off because there was nothing wrong with the item. I told them the store guaranteed me it would be removed/refunded. Its issues like this that caused me to end our relationship with T-Mobile. Customer beware.

If I could rate it zero stars I would.  The Germantown WI location has the rudest employees.  After being treated terribly by Manager Joseph I got up to leave and I tripped on the rug and he laughed at me.  He was so disrespectful.  Do not go to this location

UPDATE 2025-07-21
T-Mobile reached out to me concerning my Better Business Bureau (BBB) complaint within 24 hours and they removed the remaining amount from my balanced they claimed I owned. I received an apology for this mess of a situation. This was a nightmare of a situation, but it says something that upper management will take the time to correct issues like this once they are made aware. Updating review from 1 to 3 stars.

2025-07-13
I ordered a T-mobile internet wireless device and it was never delivered even though UPS claimed it was. T-mobile refused to speak to me, the customer, without driving to a T-mobile store so I had to do that to speak to a T-mobile business account manager. After drive to T-mobile multiple times, they finally were able to order a new internet wireless device. To make a long story short, the device got horrible reception where my business was located so I had to cancel the account to proceed to return the device. I cancelled my account within the money back guarantee trial period (for when I received the 2nd device) and T-mobile is refusing to refund me $30 and a $7 late fee. I told them they can take this up to arbitration as I am not paying it. We'll see where that goes, but I have had to drive to the T-mobile store each time to talk to their business account managers. I am floored that T-mobile doesn't care about customer service to the point where they won't concede a mere $37 charge when their whole process has been an utter nightmare.
T-mobile is a nightmare for business accounts so avoid at all costs.

After 29 yrs with Verizon, we switched to T-Mobile for cheaper international plan. A MAJOR downgrade. Always a delay reading voicemail. It spends sometimes minutes, stating it's still transcribing. With Verizon reading voicemails was instant.  The same with downloading photos. They remain blurry with sometimes more than a minute before they download clearly. Also many dropped calls, and areas with no service. And no, it is not the phone, as my wife has the same problems with a different phone. Far more than with Verizon.  I will definitely switch back!

This is for Matt at the Loveland, CO. Store🔥🔥👍🏻😎🔥🤘🏻🔥🔥
Again after helping me literally the last minute to closing with a smile and a happy to help attitude? In a hurry to let em lock up i left my wallet there and they called right away so i could pick it up in the morning. Thanks to Matt and rest of crew🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥

I called them to cancel a line they keep charging me on month after month.  A line they created by mistake.  After talking to someone for about 30 minutes I get transferred to someone else.  They want to troubleshoot a line that they created by mistake.  They hang up on me because I'm not allow to get upset that they continue to ignore what I called for.  DON'T RECOMMEND THIS COMPANY.

Has anyone else had issues canceling a “free” T-Mobile line? I’ve been going in circles with customer service trying to remove an inactive promo line that I never use — and I’m still getting charged. Each rep says something different, but nothing gets resolved. I explore you to think twice before you start services with T-mobile. I’ve incurred 75 dollars for the past 5 months and have failed multiple visits with in store and phone customer care.

I have been with T-Mobile for several years. I was very satisfied with everything until just recently when prices started going up. But I love the phones and I loved how you could get new phones on a regular basis.
My dad told me about this one phone service that is Uber cheap with great options and service. So I checked them out and decided to go with them. I called them and signed up and did everything I needed to do. They told me I needed the four digit pin from T-Mobile in order to transfer my number. So I contacted T-Mobile and got the number and they said nothing about doing anything else. The new service said it would be up to 72 hours before T-Mobile would release my phone number and I would have service. Now it’s been Almost to the fifth day with no phone service. I just got done on chat with T-Mobile with a female whose name starts with a G. She told me multiple times that I would have to call customer service or go to a store to finish doing their side of things of porting my number to the new carrier. Well, I’m disabled because I was shot in the head twice. I can’t drive and am at home most of the time except for doctors appointments and stuff. I have a habit of passing out and hitting the floor because of health issues. So it’s extremely important that I have a phone available at all times. I told her all this and More. She continued to tell me that I needed to call customer service and go to the store and that she understood my situation when actually she has no clue of my situation. I continually asked her what she was gonna do to fix things because T-Mobile was the one who caused the problem in the first place as far as no phone for now almost 5 days. They also have dissolved my account so I can’t login and contact customer service that way either. We went back-and-forth for several minutes and she just became frustrated and it was obvious. She offered no resolution and only kept telling me to call customer service when I have absolutely no way to do so. I asked her if I was gonna have to take this to the next level. When I said that she quickly ended the conversation.
I would highly suggest not getting T-Mobile because if you end up having any issues, they avoid fixing them if they are the ones that cause them. They also don’t care if you are seriously disabled and they cause you to go without a phone for 5+ days.

For 3 yrs they told me i was in their "coverage area".. i had  wifi,  and that was the only way i had any phone service. I contacted them monthly,  once upgrading my service pkg because that would help?? Whatever..They told me to troubleshoot my phone constantly.  I was finally eligible  for an upgrade.. got a new phone.. still not even a bar of  service. So pissed. There was nothing wrong with my phone.  It was a carrier problem.. i went to the local t-maybe, and dude said "welcome to at&t" wth.. there are no straight answers,  its not a network is a notwork..The best coverage..nationwide.. lies. Thats false advertising.  💯 and their customer service is total garbage. After finally porting my# to diff carrier. I get a letter saying that i owe them $96. Im reality they owe me for the past 3 years of overcharges. At one point my phone bill was $245. Whilst having no network coverage.. this is a money hungry network that doesn't care if u can get calls or not.  No one cares.  It's ur problem
. It's a phone problem.  It's everything but a network problem. So sick of it. After finally porting my #, i get a collection notice today for $96..nope. in fact they owe me reparations for the last 3 yrs of total crap. They are the worst.

Unbeknownst to us, we went to a 3rd party store to become new customers. We received new phones in the process of transferring our numbers from ATT. So far so good. Problems arose when I decided to upgrade to a phone with larger storage. Because stores do not carry larger storage, the store told me to order it online- no assistance in doing that. I went through customer service to get the phone making certain that I paid the difference to keep our bill lower and also making certain that all promotions transferred to the new phone as we were within the 14 day change window. I was assured that all would transfer. After receiving the phone, I went to return it to the store as instructed. The store said I needed to get a shipping label and return it myself. Upon calling customer service, they stated that phones purchased in a store needed to be returned to a store. The store dismissed me and refused. I went to a corporate store where they did return it with a $70 restock fee! Next, upon viewing my bill, T-Mobile did not transfer the free phone promotion! Remember, this is all in the 14 day window as advised to carry over promotions. I had to make yet another call to customer service. Luckily they corrected to issue, but I will be paying for the phone for 1 or 2 billing cycles until the issue corrects upon which time I was told I would be compensated for those 2 payments. We’ll see-won’t we. When our 2 years is complete, we are leaving T-Mobile. 
To top it off-we just received a notice that an unknown device signed in to our account. I have been trying unsuccessfully to change password. Remember, all of this has occurred within 13 days of signing up with TMobile. Huge mistake.

I gave this company a rating of "1" because there is no lower digit. I was a T-Mobile customer for over a year. During that time, I experienced terrible phone service - representatives who could not fix a problem, no receiving or making phone calls, even from my  home, no wi fi /internet service if not using my home internet, and the list goes on. I was making calls to the company at least once per week. I hate switching from one to another so I tried to keep the service. To get $5.00 off the cost, I signed up for automatic payments. BIG MISTAKE. 
In April 2024, I changed to a new bank and changed my payment method on the T-Mobile app, after my payment for April 4th was subtracted. Around April 23rd, I changed my billing information on the T-Mobile app. I got a message that I would need to pay my May's payment on the website since the payment method change could not be made before the payment date of May 4th, 2024. I also got a text message that the change was made. I confirmed it on the app. I made the May payment online and got my confirmation.
On June 4th, I got a text message as usual, that my payment was made. About a week following the receipt of "Payment received" notice, I got another text about my check was returned - or something to that matter. When I called and asked for clarification, I got a whole lot of nonsense, since no one could tell me anything that I could understand. I explained the who payment method situation to them over and over. At least three people acknowledged that the payment method was changed on the app from April. I then asked which bank returned the "check" and was told that it was the bank of the closed account. After trying for days to negotiate the canceling of the late payment, which I believe was $25.00, I paid it along with the phone payment. That was certainly a T-Mobile error. Oh, and although everyone I spoke with saw the new payment method on the site, after I complained to one of their "supervisors", they removed the new payment method from the site.
At that point I decided it was time to stop doing business with that "crocked" company.  I searched for another phone company, while taking pictures, making videos, and copying my information from the site. On June 2, 2024, I called T-Mobile and got the information requested by my new phone company to make the change. I had everything else. That made the customer representatives furious. One even told me that I don't have to make the change until the 5th of the month, after knowing full well that the billing date was the 4th. By then I had collected all my pertinent information, including payment history.
Months after, I began getting letters in the mail from T-Mobile telling me that I must make payment. I sent the first letter back with a scribble across informing them that they are violating my character. Three or four letters followed. Recently, I started receiving calls and text messages from Collection agencies stating that they are calling on behalf of T-Mobile. (See Below) 
The letters started coming shortly after I reported an internet service to Florida Consumer Affairs, unknowing that the company has "friends" in the office. I plan to write a review regarding that company also.
T-Mobile mobile service is among the WORST of the Worst. 
Anyone with information of how I can get this off my record, please leave me a message.

***See collection latter below.****
Credence Resource Management, LLC is a Debt Collector. MY NAME offer to resolve your T-Mobile account XXXX5107 with a balance of $105.76 for only $52.88 if your payment is received in our office by 06/28/2025. This is a savings of $52.88. T-Mobile will not pursue any collections on the remaining balance. You also have the ability to pay in parts. Call @ 844-978-1804 or Pay: (Link deleted). Rights: (Link deleted). Reply STOP to stop.

***The $52.88 was the amount from the first letter. That was my last payment to them when they said my check was returned. I have video of my payment history.

Worst Experience with an ISP We've Had to Date

From the Top:
Semi Aggressive salesmen (2-20 year olds) came to our place of business offering wireless internet via a data connection. Due to my frustrations with Spectrum's poor service, I figured I'd give the free trial a shot and see if we could switch over to TMobile. During the account setup, the sales rep told me I have to use the business owner's name, even though he has very little to do with operations and won't be managing the account. He assures me that I will be able to handle any account actions that I need to. 

Fast forward to the product:
We receive the router and it just can't keep up with our internet usage. We used it for maybe a max total of 2 days with one device connected to it using very little internet except to intermittently check available download/upload speeds. After day 2 we initiate the return with customer service bcause our rep wouldn't answer the phone.

The Return:
Customer service sent us a shipping label through email and it was very easy to return the unit. Once it left our building, I expected to be done with the ordeal because this was a free trial afterall. 

The Aftermath:
Roughly 2 weeks after I shipped the return, I receive an invoice in the mail for the service. It incorrectly lists the dates of use as a total of 7 days and 22GB of data. We didn't have the device for even 2 full days and there is no way we even came close to a GB of data. This invoice is blatantly incorrect. On top of that, it is charging us an almost insulting amount of $27 for said usage. On a free trial.
I call my rep, surprise, no answer. I call customer service and after 3 transfers, 2 disconnects, and 40 minutes of my time, I learn that I need the owner to do anything with the account. Which is strange because I didn't need them to set it up. If security is so important to them, then why is it only in the sales phase that they are willing to overlook security issues. I would have never even subscribed to the free trial if I knew I'd need a disconnected individual to change what I was able to do myself.

I had mobile service with t-mobile for an ongoing five years Never been late never missed a payment and now six years and still a loyal customer. I decided to take on the promotion plan they offered and added two more devices this is the worst provider ever and I do mean worst I don't know what happened but I can't wait to leave t-mobile I wish I never laid eyes on this site I can't believe I'm singing this song but it's true. My experience has become a living nightmare with them I gets nothing except lie after lie after lie with these so called reps after they wing you in with their promos  a ninety day grace period is the trap they lock you in with the devices while changing your data plan and payment amount without your consent nor contacting you at all while stealing your data and continuously overcharging you an outrageous amount for data that you never have access to. The reps have a solution for every lie they are rude and poorly trained while scamming their customers. Stealing stealing stealing from customers a multi million dollar company a deliberate scam and lies no means of curteousy what so ever. I will never recommend t-mobile to anyone I have already told others to stay away and now I am telling you. STAY AWAY!!!  YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED

First of all they raised my rate by around $30 within 2 months of switching to them this past winter. Most recently they declined a payment because a deposit I made had not cleared. My mistake for not checking my bank account first. They then proceeded to permanently block my bank account and debit card and disconnect my service. The only solution now is for me to go withdraw money from my bank and then go to a T-Mobile store to make a payment every month. This on top of being hit with a $20 fee. Completely unacceptable.

I have been with T Mobile for nearly 5 years now. I have financed and Paid off 3 devices on my account. All of a sudden i cannot finance and their reasoning is some technical bull. Long story short i am switching over to Verizon!
The best mobile operator!

My elderly mom changed from Metro to T-Mobile because they sweet talked her in a Sam's store, now she is under a 2 year contract and double payment amount. She can't pay her bill till the 3rd of the month and now they turned her phone off you can't go to a local store because they can't help you, then when she tries to call 611 she can't get any help, this is utter ridiculous.  ONLY way you can get help is call T-Mobile and she just can't figure it out, I'm out of state and when I call it's says they don't recognize my number. So frustrated please please take my advice go to another service provider.

T-Mobile treats my data like it’s a cheap thrill, sending me four defective phones under warranty. They bombard me with the same annoying ad on loop while I’m stuck on hold, enduring obnoxiously loud hold music that could wake a coma patient. They force me to request a callback to avoid long wait times, only to slap me back on hold for an hour as soon as they call. They employ clueless staff who can barely address issues. Every visit to their store is a nightmare. Employees act like they’re under the influence, taking two hours to hand over a simple SIM card because they’re too busy chatting like high school cliques. Their network security is as flimsy as tissue paper; my phone’s been compromised on their system. This company is an absolute disaster, and I can’t fathom why anyone would pay $50 per line when you can get better service practically anywhere else for half the price. You have to TRY to be this ignorant.

I called T-Mobile cell phone customer service and of course it went directly to the Philippines. It was difficult for me to communicate with the man who answered But I told him that I needed to review to charges on my bill that each was 'Default' One charge was $4.07 the other charge was $1.84 I asked him why it was a default charge after being put on hold twice for several minutes He came back and told me they were taxes and fees and I said it doesn't tell me what the taxes and fees are He put me on hold again and came back and said the $1.84 was an actual tax and the $4.07 was for fish that T-Mobile charges I asked him what the fees are and he couldn't tell me I asked for manager and again waited twice for several minutes He said he couldn't find one. I was a long time T-Mobile customer they went up on their price from $50 to 55 so I switch to boost Mobile and was not happy with them and after a month went back to T-Mobile for a plan that was $5 less and within my budget And I pay my bill through automatic bill pay every month to save $5 however with the taxes and fees my bill is now $55.91. In addition to the cost per month when I switch back to them I keep going through dead zones and my call gets disconnected This never happened before with T-Mobile but now it's happening. So I will be looking to switch to another carrier. Being sent to T-Mobile customer service in another country, being put on hold, requesting to speak to a supervisor never happened and the explanation of the charges was like pulling teeth from this customer service person. 
T-Mobile for this experience doesn't even deserve one star!

I am currently unemployed and struggling to keep even basic bills paid. I spoke with T-Mobile 3 days ago, on July 1st, and an offshore representative offered a solution by giving me a credit to my account stating that if I could also pay $27.05 that would take care of an upcoming payment arrangement installment of $54.10. He assured me the solution would cause the upcoming installment, for July 4th, to "drop off."

I confirmed multiple times it would be all I would need to do and that no more money would be owed until the following week, when I will have income to be able to pay it. I thought it was resolved. I trusted him. I believed him. 

Fast forward to today, July 4th, and T-Mobile is trying to process another payment of $54.10. I have 53 cents in my checking account until next week. I called T-Mobile and spoke to yet another offshore representative who just continued to say she understood, and she heard me, and all we needed to do to take care of things was provide a payment of $54.10 today. 

When I asked to speak to a supervisor she came back on the line and said if that had been an option she would have offered it to me initially but it is not an option. I just need to pay the $54.10.

This is so ridiculous, so cringe, and so street thug! WHAT COMPANY conducts business by baiting a long-term customer with the promise of a resolution by getting them to make a small payment only to extort them 3 days later for even more money? Why lie? Does T-Mobile need that $27.05 so badly that they are willing to teach their offshore reps to lie just to get that tiny amount? Seriously? 

I don't know any other company that does this crap. I am done with T-Mobile. I just borrowed $54.10 from a friend of mine and I paid the overdue installment because I am unemployed and cannot afford to be without my phone for an entire week. 

I still owe T-Mobile $83 or so. They will never see that money. Next Friday when I have some money I will be switching carriers. T-Mobile can choke on their thug tactics, their extortion, their lies, and the balance that I owe them. 

WORST COMPANY EVER.

I used to absolutely love TMobile. Myself and my family have used them a handful of time over the last 20 years. However, over the last year every single interaction I have had with their customer service has been abysmal. First they stick me with a phone financing plan that I had never agreed to and told me to eat the cost. When they did that, I cancelled part of my service with the intent to eventually fully get rid of my services. It turns out when this was done the service representative did not fully check my account and different account setting got changed. My email and primary contact was changed to another number on the account that I do not have access to. It was discovered in July when I went to add other services that my account was essentially bricked so I could not receive assistance without going to the store because they could not verify my account with their one time passcode despite me knowing all of my other account info including the account passcode. I went into the store to have this fixed and had been told I could receive an account credit for the inconvenience. However, this promise was never honored. The people I had spoken to were extremely rude. I decided to transfer some services to the individual who was using them and cancel the rest of my account no longer working with TMobile. They couldn’t even do that correctly. They transferred one of the two lines and did not cancel the other service that I had just signed up for. They charged me the additional shipping cost and for the less than one day I had the device. They are also charging me the full month for the other two transferred lines despite them only being active on my account for about a week of the billing period. I would never recommend TMobile to anyone again. While the prices may be good the customer service is absolutely trash. They don’t care about treating customers right at all and that is a huge difference from how they used to be. It is because of that that I will never work with them again.

Ridiculous!  I've been paying 100$ a month for the plan with yearly upgrades. Im now eligible and they want me to pay over 600$ for my upgrade. Wtf is the point of paying 100$ for specialized plan to upgrade sooner and you have to pay more to upgrade than 99% of phone companies charge to get the phone and plan initially!  "Yea u can upgrade cuz you have the most speedy plan that gives u yearly upgrades but u have to pay over half the phones MSRP!!" WILL BE IMMEDIATLY SWITCHING COMPANIES. TMobile is a JOKE.

Went to set up my phone in the Bristol store about 6 weeks ago. Aries I believe the kids name was, 1)tried to sell all sort of things for my 2 year old IPhone 12 pro.2).Then he put the wrong address down for billing.3) Found out when I got home he put me in for $14.95 a month phone protection.HUH? The whole deal went south so I went back to the store and cancelled. 2 weeks later my moms house a bill for $96! Called CS. Gave them my correct address for a ZERO balance. 2 more weeks later another $14.95 bill comes in. Another CS call another promise of a letter. 2 weeks later guess what? I go to the store and they are blown away. "You have no account, no phone number with us..this is for a protection plan." Yes your employee did this. Julio fixed it, did not print it out but I took a screen shot. I bet good money it's not over. DO NOT TRUST CUSTOMER SERVICE HERE!
Needed to get an old cell unlocked but they refused to do unless I payed my current bill even though I've only been with them for 14 years

I am a senior citizen, and normally purchase prepaid SIM cards in the amount of $15 or $25, depending on my need, at the time. I went to two authorized T-Mobile dealers in Renton, WA - the first one told me their computer system was down for prepaid cards (but evidently working for post-paid service), and directed me to another dealer in Renton. There, I was told they no longer sold $15 or $25 cards, but could sell me a $40 prepared card, along with a $10 SIM card, plus taxes and fees, which would total approximately $60. I was then offered a senior discount plan which included unlimited talk, text and data. I inquired about the cancelation fee, and was told I could cancel any time, and that the amount of the bill would be pro-rated. I confirmed the information with the manager. It seemed like a good offer, so I accepted. 

In late May or early June 2025, I stopped in an actual T-Mobile store, (not an authorized dealer), and spoke with the manager, who told me they indeed sell prepaid SIM cards in the amount of $15 and $25, and that the authorized dealer was probably trying to make a bit of extra money.

I recently ported my phone number to another provider in order to reduce my expenses. I called T-Mobile on two separate occasions prior to this to ensure I would receive a pro-rated amount once I cancelled my account; both representatives told me I would be able to do so.

My number was ported yesterday, which meant I had three days of service with T-Mobile for the current billing cycle. I called T-Mobile's customer service department this morning and was told I would receive a bill for $65 at the end of the cycle. I then spoke with a manager who told that I would have not only have to pay the full amount for this billing cycle, but would have to pay for another month's service on top of that; basically paying for 2 months with no service. This is in direct conflict with what I was told - both by the manager I spoke with at the store, and the two customer service representatives I previously spoke with.

I have written a letter to my ombudswoman asking for feedback on how to dispute the charges. I will write to my elected officials if she is unable to help. 

I have been a T-Mobile customer for years. Their lack of integrity is beyond me.

I spent 7 hours trying to activate two phones. I ended up with two phones without network connections. I was passed from rep to rep, each as inept as the one prior. They seemed disinterested in helping me solve my problem. I finally gave up. Verizon fixed the phones the next day.

Your international connections sucks frankly.   Particularly for tablets.  Like you are just trying to say you offer the service but the international is so slow that in reality it’s like having no service.   Very frustrating and disappointing from an otherwise mighty company.

I've had 10 years of good service from T-Mobile and now I'm seriously thinking about switching.

I setup a bank account a few months bank to auto charge and get their discount. Worked fine.

This month my bank notified me that it failed to be charged. I went into my account on their website and it told me they could not charge my bank (no explanation).

I tried to call, and talked 4 different reps, who all tried to explain non-sensical reasons and solutions like:
- The reason it failed was because it takes 2-3 months for new bank accounts to be enrolled in autopay (untrue, and it had been enrolled for months
- It was because there was unavailable funds in the account (untrue, and can prove it, and doesn't explain why I can't add one)
- To add my payment method, I need to remove all payment methods (untrue, tried it anyway, didn't work)
- The 4th rep when asked for my pin (which I don't have -- as I didn't have with the other 3), I asked for a one-time pin, which every other rep had given me. The 4th rep said I *needed a pin* in order for her to send me a pin. She didn't understand how this was backwards.
- The 3rd rep tried to get me to send a payment through her for an additional $10 (absolutely refused on principle of paying more money for a bug in their system)
- She then tried to send me off to another account, when I asked about the supervisor, she put me on hold, then finally said she was transferring me to a supervisor. This in fact just transferred me to a 4th rep who had no idea what was going on.

As much as I've deduced from speaking and going through it all:
- Their bank account connector (Trustly) no longer works with Mercury Bank accounts or Choice Financial Group
- They silently removed anyone with this bank from their payment methods, and silently failed payments, giving you a late payment with no explanation
- They have a bug on their website that after you try to add an invalid bank, you cannot add other payment methods (it gets stuck with this error on any payment method)

I asked for a supervisor after the first 30 minutes, and after the first rep failed to understand my problem for another 30 minutes finally said I just need to talk to a supervisor. I waited another 10 minutes, then they hung up me.

Spent over 2 hours with *no* information from them. All my information was from my own trial and error.

> Title: Misleading promotions and overpriced service

I’ve been a T-Mobile customer with two active lines, and I recently tried to get an iPhone 16 Pro Max based on their advertised “free or discounted” phone offers.

To my surprise, the reality was far from the promise. Without trading in a phone or adding a new line, they expect you to pay the full device price ($1,199) over 24 months — on top of the regular $170/month service fee for two lines.

Over two years, that totals well over $5,000 for just two lines and one phone. That’s not a deal — that’s a trap.

These offers are highly misleading, and unless you read every small detail and add lines you don’t need, you’ll end up paying way more than expected.

Please don't call your plans "discount plans" or "free phones." In third world countries, to attract more customers, they reduce the total amount paid, not double it like you do.

I was a customer of tmobile for 13 years and had to go to another carrier as the reception from my phone was getting worse and worse over a 2 year period.   so around a year and a half to two years after I had left them and 6 months after I signed back up with them, November 2024 and I had left tmobile on september 2022,  I get a hit on my credit report by a collector for $495.00 , I call mobile repeatedly and spoke with 7 reps over a month and a half period. Not one rep new what it was for.  Not one rep could expain it.   They said they would escalate it and get back to me and non every did,.  Now after 2 months still no answer as to what it is for.  Team moble and my payment of $80 per month would never ever allow someone to not pay a bill for up to 6 months.  I called the collector. I started to explain and they would not take my verbal information to check in on what the bill was for.   Something is very wrong there. Tmoble has affected my credit with no explaination of the added on $495  Buyer Beware.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for 20+ years. The last couple years things seem to go under with price and services. With all the price increase, Now they are removing their plans. I appreciated the service while I was with them, but their policies around number porting are extremely disappointing and feel predatory. Once you port your number to another carrier, they immediately cut off access to your account and all services, including things like Netflix and T-Life perks, but still charge you for the full billing cycle. In my case, I switched just two days into the new billing period and was still billed for the entire month, despite having no access to the services I paid for. I had been seriously considering returning to T-Mobile in the future, but after this experience, I definitely won’t.

I don't normally bash companies on here but this is completely ridiculous. T-Mobile is the absolute worst company and I highly recommend that NO ONE do business with them. I purchased the Galaxy A14 5G through T-Mobile, and it has been one of the most disappointing tech purchases I've ever made. The phone is incredibly slow, frequently freezes, and struggles even with the most basic apps and tasks. The screen responsiveness is poor, and overall performance feels subpar.

I chose Samsung expecting reliability, and I trusted T-Mobile to offer decent devices — but unfortunately, this model feels like a serious downgrade. It's frustrating to use, and not worth the nearly $200 I paid. I would not recommend this phone to anyone, and I sincerely hope both Samsung and T-Mobile take customer feedback like this seriously.

Zero stars!  I was a sprint customer, then t-mobile, for over 20 years, and the last 6 months has been AWFUL.  They LIED to me repeatedly.  First, I was told that I'd receive a $200 prepaid card if I signed up for home internet.  That never came, then when I asked about it, they told me I was not eligible, even after the rep on the phone said I'd receive it.  Then I was told I could trade my old phone in for a credit toward a new phone (after clearly stating that I did not want a new line).  They mail me the new phone, then tell me I have to have a new line, and it's too late to return the phone, and they won't make me pay for the new line.  A month later, I get a message saying I'm no longer eligible for the promotion, and now I have to pay for the new line I never wanted.  I'm not sure why they think it's ok to LIE over and over again.  They refuse to send a follow up email putting anything in writing, so I have no recourse, no way to prove that I've been lied to over and over again.  Wildly frustrating.

T-Mobile should rebrand Customer Service as Customer Harassment
Even as a 27+year loyal customer to Sprint/T-Mobile, it seems that getting decent customer service even for the most basic purposes at T-Mobile is no longer realistic. I had to have a phone unlocked for my elderly, almost blind and partially deaf father, and so drove to their center at Boston’s SouthBay shopping center. After waiting for a while to speak to someone, I explained my need and was promptly told that I needed to call customer service. I therefore called customer service and spoke with a Customer Service agent (on the other end of the Pacific) who ran through a full range of options to press different buttons on the locked phone, none of which worked. He then had me repeat the entire set of options to press different buttons (not sure if he was hoping for a miracle, but no such luck). He then proceeded to connect me with a Nokia (the phone’s manufacturer)representative. Someone called Kanika from Nokia appeared for a few seconds and then promptly disappeared. While T-Mobile’s customer agent claimed to be retrying to connect with Kanika, the next thing after another agonizing wait, was he put me on to another T-Mobile agent named Shane and disconnected himself. Shane was also at the other end of the Pacific in a very noisy call center, where I could hear loud chatter and laughter which often drowned our conversation. Shane put me through exactly the same steps yet again as with the last agent, all for nought. Ironically, Shane’s next recommendation was that I go to a T-Mobile store, while I was still on the phone with her. I explained to Shane that that was indeed the first thing I did and it was the store that asked me to call customer service, and I was not about to head to a store again, having lost so much time!
Shane also connected me again with Kanika from Nokia, who was exquisitely unhelpful, asking about a whole number of ridiculous questions such as the invoice number of the purchase, and the source of the purchase even though I had the most relevant information on the phone from the phone’s own label. Thereafter, Kanika said she could not help me and disconnected herself. 
Shane then wanted me to speak with her supervisor, and so put me on hold yet again, finally connecting me with her supervisor Maria and explaining that she had provided Maria with all the background on the situation. That said, Maria’s opening salvo was, “What happened?” asked with an air of such naivete that I wondered if we were playing ‘Babes in the Wood’ in real life. I told Maria categorically, that I was not going to repeat the entire saga of events for her, and if she was not able to do something constructive, I was going to end the call. Maria then said that for her to do something constructive, I would have to go back to the T-Mobile store again. That was more than enough for me- after 3.5 hours, dealing with a bunch of totally incompetent T-Mobile professionals, I had no solution. Fancy that more than 27 years of customer loyalty produced such appalling customer service!
Shame, T-Mobile!

If I could leave zero stars, I would.

I opened 4 phone lines and ordered 4 wearables in December 2024. The store said the wearables were out of stock and I could pick them up by March. Despite this, they began charging me for them in January—an extra $100 per month over what I was quoted.

They claimed discounts don’t apply in the first month, but the charges kept coming for five months. I went to the store regularly, and every time I was told “the rep isn’t in” and would call me. No one ever did.

Finally in June, they removed the wearables from my account, but I still have no refund, no credit, and no answers about the extra payments I made.

Worst customer service I’ve experienced. Avoid this location completely.

I had to switch phone provider to get a package deal with my internet provider. So when I canceled my bill with T-Mobile, they sent me 2 portions, the time I used their service, and the other portion where I was with new provider. I called and confirmed with rep that I should only pay for the time that I was with T-Mobile. Rep confirmed and said yes, only pay for that portion. I called back to pay and confirmed with second rep that I don't need to pay the remaining amount when I was with the new provider already. She confirmed yes, no need to pay $25.50 when you already paid the portion for your service. Following month, I still got the bill for that amount plus late fees. I called back and confirmed with rep about my balance. He advised me that my balance is $0 and I could ignore that statement as it should have been $0. I confirmed over and over with that rep. He said yes, don't worry about that statement, it's $0 balance for me. The following month, I got another statement with more late fees on top of that. I called back, and now they said they don't have records of anyone noting that they advised me of $0 balance, only me inquiring about my balance. I told them to listen to the recordings if their reps didn't note correctly. They refused to do that and said I owed that amount. I refused to pay based on the principle that I didn't use their service during that time, and I already paid with the new provider, and that their reps had advised me of $0 balance more than once, as I did pay for the portion that I'd used their service for. Then they sent my account to collection agencies, I disputed, then they sent to another one, again and again. Now the balance is $73.98 from a mere $25.50. Mind you I've been a loyal customer with them for 19 years, where I was never late or missed any payment, and the second I had to switch provider for better deal, I was thrown out with no care or concerns for my account. after 19 years now you want to triple a balance that wasn't supposed to be there in the first place? what a THIEF!!

They promised never to raise my rate on magenta 55. They did. Told me everyone got increased. That was the reason I left the big cable company.  T-mobil lied just like they do to. I'm all about honesty and loyalty. Beware t- mobile doesn't care!!!

Scammers are using your company to make fraudulent transactions!   I’ve found over £250 charged to my son’s Tmobil bill (PS Games). Like many others, my son has been scammed by a company named BOKU. Tmobile and PlayStation are not doing anything to stop this company. I called them on the number PlayStation provided but only had a silly answering service. T Mobile is not doing anything about these fraudulent activities. How about an alert before the transaction is made rather than letting scammers have a field day?

I have been a T-Mobile customer for many years broken up by a few years with AT&T. We came back to T-Mobile because of the good customer service and also a great plan for seniors. My first question is why in the world did you move customer service out of the U.S. and put it in Asia? The agents are difficult to understand, they don't give good support, and my experience was most unsatisfactory. I called about upgrading my phone, told him I needed to keep my billing as low as possible. My plan was $70 and I was looking at adding $12.50 per month for an S25 after trading in my S22. Somehow, he changed my plan to Essentials plus $15 for the phone. Now my bill is almost $20 more per month instead of just $12.50 more. It's just so annoying to get screwed after being a loyal customer for so many years. I am frustrated, disappointed, resentful, and definitely not satisfied with your company.

I stopped in to the T-Mobile store located at 6216 US-6 W, Portage IN 46368 to get a replacement SIM card for my iPad, as the data service hadn’t been working recently. For years, I’ve used a SIM tied to my old phone number in that device because I didn’t want to lose the number.

The rep told me I now have to pay $25 more per month as an add-on. When I asked why, his response was, “Well, you got away with it for this long — I guess they figured it out.” That’s hardly a professional explanation. I then asked if there was any way to reduce the cost, and he told me it’s actually $65 more unless I settle for just 3GB of data for $10.

When I expressed my frustration and mentioned I’d be sharing this on social media, the T-Mobile rep told me to leave the store — or he would have me removed.

Is this really how T-Mobile treats a military veteran and 25-year loyal customer? Very disappointed.
Please avoid at all cost
I will never pay for a membership again! So expensive

Was on the phone with T-Mobile at least 4 to 5 times for a total of 5 hours. I was promised three times by the agents and one of them was a supervisor that they would call me back and they never did. I was calling because I found out that I was being charged for a defective Samsung watch and I've been paying on it for almost 2 years without service as they did cancel the service but they didn't stop charging me for the watch like they promise they would. How you been told them if we didn't get it straightened out immediately I would call the better Business bureau FTC and FCC. I filed two complaints one with the better Business bureau in the other with FTC and within a day I got response back from the better Business bureau after explaining my situation they sent a letter to T-Mobile and I got one of their top level people and we were able to settle the issue quickly. 
To be honest their customer service model that they use I don't believe is morally right and some people don't have the time to mess with trying to get things fixed with the low level reps they hire. I can tell you the easiest thing I would recommend if you're not getting to the top and need to it's just contact the BBB. They really did their job and helped me. I was given credit for all the charges that they had charged me applied to my bill but there was no going above and beyond with any extra freebies for my lost time and frustration but I did get a formal apology. 
Thanks BBB!
Friend told me you need to demand a American speaking citizen from America.

I had a very frustrating experience at a T-Mobile branch. I went in to report issues with my service, only to find out my estranged wife had deactivated it due to our ongoing divorce. I urgently needed a working phone before traveling abroad and asked if my existing phone could be converted to a prepaid plan. Instead, I was pressured into buying a expensive new phone and extras I didn’t need.

The staff failed to transfer my data or properly set up the new phone so I need to returned two days later, and the assistant manager claimed everything was fixed — but once I left the country, the phone still didn’t work. I couldn’t call or message during an important business trip, which caused major problems.

When I came back and tried to return the phone, I was told it was too late since it was already 19 days due to their 14-day return policy — even though the phone was never fully usable from day one UP to 10 days of purchase. The assistant manager even threatened to kick me out of the store if I didn’t leave, considering I’m disable. 

This was one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve had. I expected better from T-Mobile since we were customers of Tmobile for more than 15 years, especially in a situation where quick and honest support was clearly needed.

T-Mobile Customer Service

I’ve never had such a consistently frustrating experience with customer service as I have with T-Mobile. Every time I call, the wait time is anywhere from 30 minutes to over an hour. When I finally reach a representative, the communication is incredibly difficult—there's often a heavy accent, and I find myself repeating the reason for my call multiple times with little to no resolution.

Most recently, I contacted T-Mobile to confirm the shipping address for a replacement phone. Ironically, since I don’t currently have a phone, I couldn’t receive the verification code they insisted on texting me—despite the fact that the whole reason for my call was that I don’t have a working phone. I explained this repeatedly, yet the representative couldn't assist and offered no alternative solution. To make matters worse, the in-store rep who processed the exchange never confirmed where the replacement would be sent, which is why I had to call in the first place.

This entire process was a complete waste of time. While the rep ended the call with “God bless you,” which I appreciated, kind words don’t make up for poor service and lack of resolution. T-Mobile support has, unfortunately, been one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve encountered.

Signed up for Internet service with T-Mobile promised a thank you gift card was supposed to have been here two months after I signed out called now it’s going to be two months after I called to set it up to find out where the gift card was and I’m sure when I call again in another month or so it’s gonna be another two months from the time I called them. T-Mobile actually sucks bad they don’t know what they’re doing. Nobody knows what the hell they’re doing, but they damn sure want their money?

When i signed up for service 5 years ago I was given a PIN.  I have to verify that PIN  before I can switch to their prepaid plan.  That was 5 years ago , that was 2 hurricanes and 2 moves ago. If I would have known I would have put the number in my safe deposit box  :).  My only other option is to go to the closest tmobile store and where I live now it is a three hour drive.  Was very disappointed in the finality of the call.  I just don't get it you have a loyal customer that has NEVER been late with a payment which was $171.00 a month and they are unable to offer any  other options for me.  Going to switch carriers as soon as I can.  Beware my good people of the Trustpilot world.  When opening a new TMOBILE account get that PIN tattooed on your body cause it's like double top secret.

My A12 was acting up, like icons would move around at will, random selection of pages at will...I thought it was dying...so went and saw Angel at the store on Roosevelt Blvd in St. Pete, FL....It took an hour but she kept at it and finally her magic worked.....She was professional and even had a sense of humor.....Tremendous employee!!!!

Was a Sprint customer before they where bought by TMobile. Over 15 years (family plan with 7 lines). Good service. AT&T approached 2 months ago with some excellent pricing so we changed! After Thousands of dollars spent by us and family T-Mobile will not refund the balance of account. Company is very Petty and we are happy we changed.

Your Employee Juan is why!...
Never have I been so PLEASED with the Service, Knowledge, Information, Friendliness and Determination to help me with every question.

I had many of them and he quickly responded without hesitation.  He helped me with many things.  I kept him on the phone for an extended time because I was researching my opportunities to better myself after Many, Many Years with my existing Carrier(A*&T.)  Juan never lost patience.  He provided me with exactly what I was looking for that I was not getting from A*&T.  I was so impressed I chose to Change from A*&T without hesitation.  

I felt the service that I expected was not there anymore. 

The Products and Services of T-Mobile are very IMPRESSIVE!  

But I would not have known that without Juan's Dedication to his Job.  

It was 50% Juan and 50% T-Mobile that resulted in my Changeover!

Thanks to Juan/T-Mobile...
Steve

After 2 months of battle with T-Mobile regarding fraudulent line that was added to my account without my authorization about a year ago, T-Mobile refused to take any responsibility whatsoever, moreover, they kept charging me for another 2 months for the unknown line.
The amount is not huge, however, the level of negligence they have is unprecedented.  In fact, I suspect there are many more customers like that at tmobile. I discovered that there were 5 lines added and removed to my account over time without my authorization!   Tmobile refuses to do anything about it or maybe they add those lines intentionally to show a high number of subscribers and higher billing?! 
Anyway, this is a great case for CFPB complaint and audit. I plan to do so. Someone needs to give them a lesson.

After a horrific experience with Verizon—who terminated all our lines without warning, refused to release our numbers, and left my elderly parents completely cut off from communication—I turned to T-Mobile.

I ordered my new phone online while I was in Miami just to restore basic communication. But the real miracle happened in New York, where my elderly parents went into a T-Mobile store and were helped by Michael.

Michael was incredible. Patient, kind, and understanding, he walked my parents—who are not tech-savvy—through setting up new phones after they’d just been traumatized by Verizon. My father, who has diabetes, high blood pressure, and recently had knee replacement surgery, had his phone number of over 20 years effectively “kidnapped” by Verizon. He had no access to doctors, no way to reach family—until T-Mobile stepped in.

Michael took the time to ensure they understood every feature, answered all their questions, and treated them with respect and dignity when they needed it most. In a world where corporate abuse is far too common, T-Mobile and Michael showed us what true customer care looks like.

Thank you, T-Mobile, for giving my family peace of mind and restoring what Verizon destroyed.

MICHAEL at the Smithhaven Mall location in NY
Address: 318 Smith Haven Mall Suite 208, Lake Grove, NY 11755
La experiencia y todo el marketing son los mejores

TMobile in Tifton, GA. I went in to speak to someone in person about getting a phone plan and phone and watch... 2 reps in the store waiting on one customer.  No acknowledgement, as I stood there looking at phones and then I went into browser and looked around.  I was in there about 10 minutes then I put my phone away and left.  They didn't say goodbye or anything.  They lost about 2k in sales today from me. I'll go on line and get my phone and watch.

I requested a new phone number due to harassing unknown calls which I already blocked on their scam shield. Once I had the new phone number tmobile let thru one number I had on scam shield. I had to change my number again. Since I have had these 2 new numbers I have gotten notifications which say my numbers which were blocked on scam shield were temporarily unblocked for 2 hours. Once I click on it it brings me to a screen that indicates on a button that is in the off position of blocked numbers. I had to switch the button to block the numbers on my list. This happened twice. On one call to address this issue to their so called customer service he couldn't explain or help. He claimed he didn't understand how it could happen. Later technical support couldnt help and told me to take my phone to s tmobile store or call samsung. Why should i call Samsung when the problem is with tmobiles own scam shield? But I then receive a message on my screen about family allowances which I didn't even order nor was discussed. Besides I don't even have a family to begin with. The matter was I called regarding their scam shield. Also I was scammed in the past. I had a phone stolen and I called tmobile for a replacement but they told me it wasn't under my insurance plan because it was less than a month I owned it on my insurance. So I had to pay off the phone and they never gave me a replacement. Their customer service and technical support is rated a zero. They need to be investigated for their business practices etc. I've been with this company for several years. Once I pay off my phone I'm leaving!! Also like I said when I was on a call discussing their scam shield goof up the rep enrollment me in family allowance! I never ordered it nor were we discussing it. Also later when I checked my plan they added 30 day free 10 go smhs trial which I never ordered. I understand that someone can get into your phone! Which explains their scam shield activity. This company needs to be investigated. Next im taking this matter to bbb. BTW I have screen shots of everything. Also I've had their scam shield for some time now. It's useless. It let's in unknown text messages and calls from scammers etc and calls not on my call list all through the years!! Totally scam and a waste of money to pay for it!!

I wish I could rate this company negatively because my experience has been absolutely unacceptable. I've only been a customer for a month, and they've already tried to raise my bill by around $60 per month. I signed up for a plan featuring three phone lines and two Internet connections for $269.84, yet I received a bill over $400. When I reached out to customer service, I discovered not only that they were overcharging me, but they also wanted me to pay $303 or more per month, completely disregarding the plan that was promised to me.

I have multiple screenshots of my conversation with Angel, the representative who set up my account, clearly detailing what was offered to me. Despite this evidence, upper management and the CEO's team are dismissing my claims, insisting that I am "confused" or "misunderstanding" my plan. I've consistently shared these screenshots with various representatives—who all have access to them—but they refuse to acknowledge my case.

After paying my bill of $270, I received a call from a woman named Rosa Bojorquez. Once again, she tried to claim that my initial rate didn’t include services like insurance or Internet, despite the proof I provided. She offered me a new plan with fewer services for the same price or a higher cost for the same service I was originally promised. It’s clear that T-Mobile is trying to bully me into accepting unjustifiably higher charges, and as retaliation for my refusal, they have cut off my phone line, leaving me unable to make outgoing calls or use FaceTime. This situation is nothing short of outrageous.
T-Mobile is just a bunch of liars that won’t follow through on promotions. Thanks for coercing me into getting your internet.

text: I calling for management to fix an on going issue for the last 11 months. I moved from Verizon to T-Mobile for a better deal. I was promised $130.00 bill and 2 free phones.  Neither happened. I have talked to customer service eight times, twice to your special department that handles the kind of frustrating issue. I have been promised that they could fix it. Cancel Service.  I called in and spent 10 minutes with one person. They said oh I can't help, I will have to transfer you.  Transfer sent me to a line ringing at least 50 times and then a recording. Someone finally answered. I spent another 6 minutes with a rep asking to cancel, she then states that she can't do that and a supervisor will need to come on. Waited another 10 minutes for a supervisor. When he answered I just stated Cancel My Service.  He had to put me on hold for another 5 minutes.  This is 5/22/25 he came back stating that my service won't be cancelled until 6/20/25. What a joke.  I will not be using this service because it is terrible first of all and I already have much better internet for the same price. T-mobile has to be the worst telecommunications company on the planet next to Charter Communications.  Get it together Tmobile and quit messing with peoples time.

I have been with T-Mobile since 2020. i HAVE A WATCH LINE AND TWO CELL PHONE. i HAVE UPGRADED WATCHES TWO TIMES SAME HPONE NUMBER NO CHANGE IT SEEMS THEY HAVE BEEN CHARGING ME ADDITIONAL 12.00 A MONTH FOR A WATCH LINE FOR 5 YEARS AND WONT CREDIT MY ACCOUNT. LOOKING FOR LAWYER!! REP WANTED TO GIVE ME 144.00 TO RESOLVE, i SAID NO. LAWYER NEEDED  THESE COMPANIES NEED TO STOP STEALING FROM US.

I began being a T-Mobile customer in 2010. In 2023,I got a Moto G phone that malfunctioned, I use the phone for a year consistently rebooting everyday. In September 2023 I contacted the company regarding the phone I was upgraded to a Nord. That phone was not what I expected and did not function well. At that time T-Mobile told me I would get credit for the phone, this situation was escalated and taken to the top to be resolved finally I was informed that I would get a free phone. That did not happen I had to contact T-Mobile every month for the next 2 years up until today! Our average phone call was an hour! Finally I was tricked by T-Mobile and to agreeing to pay for the phone. I did not agree to this. I was informed by T-Mobile that they would not do anything about this situation finally. So I will port my number next week! Because I don't have to take this from this company. This is how they treat their customers who has been with them over 10 years. I have a few words that I would say to T-Mobile at this point but I will retain my dignity. Avoid T-Mobile at all costs! And be very careful when you talk to the customer service reps because they are tricky!

This review is specifically about T-Mobile Home Internet service, and the reason for this review is because of their dishonest practices.

I am sure some people may have good connectivity, but usually the character of a business shows when something goes wrong.

I moved to a rural area and 2 weeks before I moved I checked to see what Internet company I could get. I saw that T-Mobile's coverage map showed excellent coverage in the area I'm moving to, and the sales rep also told me "We definitely have coverage in that area!"

Here's another deceptive thing the sales rep did: He tried to sell me the most expensive plan, not mentioning any others.

After I did my research I found the plan I wanted and the new sales rep still wanted to sell me the most expensive plan. Despite telling her EXACTLY what I wanted, she still tried to convince me to get the most expensive one. This is a pattern that seems to show up again and again.

When I arrive at my new location they asked me who I have for cell phone service.. I said I have Google Fi which runs on T-Mobile. They looked at each other and said "uh oh." They told me there is no T-Mobile service in the area.

I looked at my phone, and it said SOS only. I had no signal whatsoever.

I told them "I did research and saw that T-Mobile works here. I ordered their home Internet." My cousin looked at me and said "Everyone here who's ever tried T-Mobile here says it sucks. You can't get service here."

I had to switch back to AT&T on my cell phone in order to have service.

I tried to hook up the router anyway, and I got one bar. Router must be stronger or something.

Still though, I got a max of like 1 megabit per second which is completely unusable.

I used up 75gb of AT&T Hotspot data in April because I could not function with the T-Mobile. And the hotspot isn't good enough for work, I had to go over my aunt's house who had Fronteir DSL to work from home.

I had planned to get Starlink so I didn't pay the bill when it came, I figured I would get a refund. But Starlink is so expensive.

I have to tell you that if I would have known T-Mobile didn't work I would have just gotten Starlink 2 weeks before I moved here and everything would have been fine, coz I had the money to do it then.

I eventually paid the $65 charge plus a $20 late fee to turn the service back on, because I was running out of hotspot data.

When I finally get Starlink I call T-Mobile customer service to cancel it. The guy would not cancel the service, he just kept trying to convince me to keep the service ad nauseam.

I could not believe it! I had to open up a BBB complaint just to cancel the service. A woman called from T-Mobile and she cancelled the service for me and apologized about the agent.

She told me (get this) that the coverage map on the website does not guarantee service in any given area, and they couldn't refund me because they saw the service had been used.

I decided to be diplomatic and just accept it. She did zero out the charge for May.

But then the BBB received a reply where T-Mobile basically called me and everyone else that lives here liars, and said that they have excellent coverage in this area and I should not have had a problem.

This is basically lying through their teeth in public record to avoid the truth coming out.

Take this as you will. Me? I will NEVER EVER be a customer of T-Mobile EVER again.
Tmobile trash bill goes up every year price lock not guaranteed wifi disconnects at anytime 😑

I have had two T-Moble prepaid phones for over 10 years. I seldom used them and kept the account active by paying a deposit on each phone every 90 days. I have almost $200 balance. On May 1st I was notified that the plan will not longer be available. I was refused a refund even though they cancelled the plan and will be refusing to give me service that I have paid for.

I have been with T-Mobile for 10 years. They used to be a reputable company. Unfortunately, their shady billing practices as of late have me questioning their reputation. I canceled the internet service late December. A few days after canceling, I ended up in the hospital for a few months. When I was released I sent back the router. They still charged me $400 for the router saying that it was sent back after the acceptable time period. Nowhere was there ever mention of a time period. I explained the situation to them but they refused to work with me. So, now they have the router AND the $400 (had to pay in order to keep service). I told them since I paid for the router they needed to send it back to me, I legally own it. They said that wasn't their policy. To me, that's the same as them stealing it AND blackmailing me. The sad part is that I am still under contract with them for another year. I will definitely be dropping them once the contract is done. Stay away!

STAY AWAY FROM T MOBILE! ZERO CUSTOMER SERVICE HELP!
